weirdFact: Adults have no functioning mouth — they emerge, mate, and die within two weeks, living entirely on stored fat.
whyItsCool: The wing tips evolved to mimic snake heads. A predator looking close sees fangs instead of a meal.

weirdFact: Its iridescence is structural, not pigment-based, and stays vivid for centuries after the beetle dies.
whyItsCool: 8th-century Japanese craftsmen pressed thousands of wings into the Tamamushi Shrine. Still glittering today.

weirdFact: Sways when it walks to imitate a leaf in the breeze. Some have fake bite marks on their bodies.
whyItsCool: Evolved camouflage so committed that even other leaf insects sometimes try to eat them.

weirdFact: Looks identical to the thorns on its host plant — including the ones right next to it.
whyItsCool: Mothers guard their nymphs and 'sing' vibration warnings through the stem when predators approach.

weirdFact: Doesn't just hide on orchids — it lures pollinators in better than real flowers do.
whyItsCool: First known animal to mimic a flower well enough to attract its prey instead of just hiding from predators.

weirdFact: Can carry 850 times its body weight — proportionally stronger than any vertebrate.
whyItsCool: Males change color with humidity. Wet morning: black. Dry afternoon: olive yellow.
